1_gaunt_cg_sparsity.description: Wigner 3j selection rules enforce angular momentum conservation in (j_a, j_b, q) coupling.

2_vertex_parity_gs_zero.description: For GS (l=0, kappa=-1): only l_b values with l_a+l_b+q odd contribute. With l_a=0, need l_b+q odd; triangle on j forces q = l_b (since j_a=1/2, need |1/2-j_b| <= q <= 1/2+j_b). For l_b values with kappa giving j_b, l_b+l_b=even => V=0.

3_so4_channel_count.description: Number of (kappa_ext, kappa_int, q) triples with nonzero coupling for each (n_ext, n_int) pair. Uses j-triangle and l-parity.

4_delta_mj_conservation.description: Sigma(a,c) = 0 when m_j_a != m_j_c, enforced by 3j selection: sum over m_q conserves m_j.

5_spatial_parity.description: All nonzero V entries have l_a + l_b + q odd (E-type parity), where l is derived from kappa.

6_furry_theorem.description: Tadpole = sum_{q,m_q} V(a,a,q,m_q)*G_gamma(q) = 0 for all a. On Dirac states, diagonal coupling V(a,a) has l_a+l_a+q = 2l_a+q; for q odd this is always odd (parity satisfied). Furry requires the 3j(j,q,j;-m,0,m) to vanish or cancel in sum.

7_ward_identity.description: ||[Sigma, H0]|| / ||Sigma|| where H0 = diag(n+1/2) = Dirac eigenvalues. For exact Ward identity this vanishes.

8_charge_conjugation.description: Self-energy is Hermitian (symmetric for real). Additionally checks kappa -> -kappa symmetry of diagonal elements.
